title: The Canal to the Five Seas
description: On pages 11–13, the canal to the five seas as the crew reaches Reverse Mountain and is dragged into the impossible currents that lead to the Grand Line.
scene: The Canal to the Five Seas. The crew reaches Reverse Mountain and is dragged into the impossible currents that lead to the Grand Line. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Then they would drive the water up the waterway.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: And at the very top, the currents will merge and come out to the Grand Line!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Since we're already riding the currents, all we have to worry about is steering properly.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: REVERSE MOUNTAIN is a winter island so the currents that hit its edge will travel down to the bottom of the sea.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: So if we fail to enter the waterway,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: We'll crash and get dragged down to the bottom of the sea. Got it?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So basically, you're saying that it's a magic mountain, right?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: I shouldn't have expected you to under- stand...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Never heard of a ship going up a mountain before.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: No but I've heard things about Grand Line...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: supposedly, half of the people die just trying to go in.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: entering it is no easy task.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I can't even see the top because of the clouds!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Get a good grip of the rudder so we don't get dragged down under!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: It's the entrance to the Waterway!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: We're veering off course! Turn a little to the right!

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on the canal to the five seas. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
